sourceMovie.title: Enemy of the State
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Enemy of the State for the same reason it broke through in 1998: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tony Scott keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Minority Report
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Enemy of the State clicked for you, Minority Report (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Enemy of the State?
faq.answer: Try Minority Report, Bridge of Spies, The Bourne Legacy—each shares DNA with Enemy of the State while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Enemy of the State?
faq.answer: Enemy of the State (1998) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Enemy of the State worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
